subject: Pipeline DQ problem patterns — design inputs for net-new system
agreement: all three teams reviewed 50+ DQ signals across CALBEAF-141 runs (Apr 23 / Apr 25 / Apr 28) and agree on this classification

body.summary: Two structural problems dominate. One signaling problem is process noise, not a data problem.

problem_1.name: Missing geo at load time
examples: DanceUS: 81% drop rate (159 events, no lat/lng)
TheRonda: 70% drop rate (713 events, no lat/lng)
problem_1.root_cause: Sources (iCal feeds, GCal, HTML scrapers) rarely embed coordinates. Current pipeline adds geo as a second pass (Booker FB enrichment or geocoder). If that pass doesn't run before load, events are silently dropped.
problem_1.narvest_design_input: Geo must be resolved at ingest time, inline — not as a deferred enrichment step. Ingest pipeline should refuse to accept an event without geo or queue it in a 'needs-geo' holding lane with retry, not silently drop it at load time.

problem_2.name: Category/duration mismatch
problem_2.frequency: common — present in majority of sources
examples: DC Capital Tangueros: 8 duration violations in one source
Tucson: 44 duration violations in one source
Prague-workshops: Tango Weekend (72h) classified as Milonga (SHORT category, cap 24h)
Prague-milongas: Marathon after-party (5.5h) classified as Marathon (LONG, min 24h)
TangoCat: Tango Week Marathon (192h) exceeds 168h LONG cap
problem_2.root_cause: Classifier assigns category from title/description keywords. Duration is not an input to classification — it's a post-load gate. Result: multi-day workshops get SHORT labels; short after-parties get LONG labels; real 8-day festivals hit an arbitrary cap.
problem_2.narvest_design_input: Duration should be an input to the classifier, not a post-check rejection. Category model should be: (keywords + duration) → category, with soft caps that flag-for-review rather than hard-reject. The 168h Marathon cap needs a FESTIVAL exception or a configurable ceiling.

problem_3.name: DQ signal volume / messaging design
problem_3.observation: 50 Porter→Harvey messages from 3 bulk runs. Apr 25 alone: 44 messages in 35 minutes, one per source.
problem_3.root_cause: Current design: Porter emits one DQ message per source per run. At scale this floods inboxes and buries signal in noise.
problem_3.narvest_design_input: DQ reporting should be batched into a single run-level digest (one message per pipeline run, not per source). Only fire if thresholds are breached. Attach per-source breakdown as payload, not as separate messages.

secondary_patterns.missing_venue_fk: Small numbers (1–4 per source) — venue registry gaps, not endemic
secondary_patterns.classifier_bugs: Zero across all 50 signals — classifier is solid
secondary_patterns.timezone_utc: Fixed in current system (2026-04-01 bug) but lesson: store Z-suffix UTC from day 1 at ingest, never convert later

body.tl_dr: Build geo into ingest (not deferred). Make duration an input to category classification (not a post-check). Batch DQ reporting to one digest per run.
